mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production signals an expansion in the manufacturing sector, driving up demand for S&P 500 stocks and producing positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in housing starts signals a reduction in supply of new homes, driving up demand for existing homes and producing positive forward returns for S&P 500 stocks over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with declining housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in initial unemployment claims signals a weakening labor market, driving down demand for S&P 500 stocks and producing negative forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with increasing initial unemployment claims are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in real disposable personal income signals an expansion in consumer demand, driving up demand for S&P 500 stocks and producing positive forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with increasing real disposable personal income are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in the S&P 500 stock price index signals an increase in supply of new equity, driving down demand for existing stocks and producing negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with increasing S&P 500 stock price index are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decrease in the unemployment rate signals a strengthening labor market, driving up demand for S&P 500 stocks and producing positive forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with decreasing unemployment rate are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decrease in the consumer price index signals a decrease in inflation expectations, driving down discount rates applied to equity cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with decreasing consumer price index are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
